name: Epinephrine (Adrenaline)
description: Endogenous catecholamine with alpha and beta adrenergic activity. First-line treatment for anaphylaxis and cardiac arrest. In anaphylaxis, IM thigh is preferred over IV (safer, equivalent efficacy). Do not delay for IV access in anaphylaxis.
dose: Anaphylaxis: 0.3-0.5 mg IM (anterolateral thigh) | Auto-injector 0.3 mg IM. Repeat q5-15 min PRN. Cardiac arrest: 1 mg IV/IO q3-5 min during CPR. Severe bronchospasm: 0.3 mg IM.
window: In anaphylaxis: give IM immediately -- do not delay for IV access.
contraindications: No absolute contraindications in life-threatening emergency
warnings: IV epinephrine for anaphylaxis should only be used in cardiac arrest or profound shock -- dangerous if given IV push to a conscious patient.
Extravasation causes tissue necrosis -- ensure IV patency.
Increases myocardial oxygen demand -- use caution in known coronary disease outside arrest.

pediatric.dose: Anaphylaxis: 0.01 mg/kg IM (max 0.5 mg). EpiPen Jr (0.15 mg) for 15-30 kg. Cardiac arrest: 0.01 mg/kg IV/IO q3-5 min.
pediatric.note: Use weight-based dosing. Broselow tape reference recommended.

obstetric.note: Use standard anaphylaxis dosing -- maternal survival is paramount. Epinephrine is safe in pregnancy for life-threatening emergencies.
